text.title: Platform Engineering and Observability Trends
text.summary: Platform engineering growth trajectory, portal vs platform distinction, golden paths; OpenTelemetry dominance, three pillars plus profiling, and shift-left observability.

section.title: Platform Engineering
section.p: Platform team adoption: 45% of large orgs in 2022 → 80% projected by 2026 (Gartner). The driver is developer experience — platform teams reduce cognitive load by providing "golden paths" that make the right thing easy.

rule.statement: Distinguish platform (backend system) from portal (frontend UI); investing in portal without platform produces expensive dashboards with no underlying capability.
rule.rationale: A common mistake: teams build Backstage (portal) without the underlying platform (Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD pipelines). The portal can only surface what the platform provides.

list.item: Platform: Kubernetes, Terraform — provisions, deploys, orchestrates
Portal: Backstage, Port — discovers, accesses, visualizes

section.p: Golden paths map directly to SDD constitution.md: define the paved road once, enforce it in quality gates. Metrics that matter: time to first commit, deployment frequency, lead time for changes, mean time to recovery.

section.title: OpenTelemetry and Observability

rule.statement: Start with OTel traces on agent task boundaries (start/end/error) before adding metrics and logs — traces provide the most value for debugging agent failures.
rule.rationale: Traces show the execution path through a system; for debugging agent failures, seeing which steps ran, in what order, and where they failed is more valuable than aggregate metrics or unstructured log lines.

section.p: OpenTelemetry is the #2 CNCF project (after Kubernetes) and the de-facto standard for vendor-neutral telemetry as of 2025.

list.item: Hierarchical: agent + gateway combined (recommended for agent pipelines)

rule.statement: Apply shift-left observability: instrument at every stage (dev → test → stage → prod), not only in production.
rule.rationale: Observability gaps in dev and test environments mean failures are first detected in production. Shift-left observability catches performance regressions and error patterns in earlier, cheaper-to-fix stages.
